items.title: Open source package with 1 million monthly downloads stole user credentials

items.summary: Hundreds of subdomains from dozens of universities have been hijacked by scammers.

items.title: In a first, a ransomware family is confirmed to be quantum-safe
items.summary: Technically speaking, there's no practical benefit to use PQC. So why is it being used?

items.summary: When authentication fails, things can go very, very wrong.

items.title: Contrary to popular superstition, AES 128 is just fine in a post-quantum world
items.summary: A stubborn misconception is hampering the already hard work of quantum readiness.

items.summary: Grinex says needed hacking resources "available exclusively to... unfriendly states."

items.summary: Here's which players are winning the race to transition to post-quantum crypto.

items.summary: Western Union exec says there were "challenges" working with Broadcom.

items.title: Supply-chain attack using invisible code hits GitHub and other repositories
